regions.name: Sensory Input
regions.shortDescription: The brain's sensory intake, where signals from your eyes, ears, and skin first arrive. (Simple: the doorway where everything you see, hear, and feel comes in.)
regions.role: Receptors convert light, sound, and touch into electrical nerve signals and send them inward for processing. (Simple: it turns what your senses pick up into brain messages and starts them moving.)
regions.exampleActivity: Light from a red apple becomes electrical signals travelling from your eyes.

regions.name: Visual Cortex
regions.shortDescription: The occipital region that decodes signals from your eyes. (Simple: figures out what you're looking at.)
regions.role: Detects edges, colour, depth, and motion, building recognisable objects out of raw visual signals. (Simple: turns eye messages into shapes and colours you can recognise.)
regions.exampleActivity: Detects that the object is round, red, and apple-shaped.

regions.name: Auditory Cortex
regions.shortDescription: The temporal region that interprets sound. (Simple: understands what you hear.)
regions.role: Analyses pitch, rhythm, loudness, and direction to turn sound waves into meaningful sounds. (Simple: turns noises into sounds you understand, like voices or a horn.)
regions.exampleActivity: Judges how loud and close a car horn is while crossing a street.

regions.name: Thalamus
regions.shortDescription: The brain's central relay station for incoming senses. (Simple: the sorter that sends each message to the right place.)
regions.role: Routes each sensory signal to the correct area of cortex and helps regulate attention. (Simple: passes messages to the right brain part so they get understood.)
regions.exampleActivity: Sends visual signals to the visual cortex and sounds to the auditory cortex.

regions.name: Hippocampus
regions.shortDescription: The memory hub that forms and retrieves memories. (Simple: saves new memories and finds old ones.)
regions.role: Binds details into memory traces and links new information to what you already know. (Simple: packs new things into memories and helps you find them again later.)
regions.exampleActivity: Matches the red object with the stored idea of 'apple'.

regions.name: Amygdala
regions.shortDescription: The brain's emotion and threat centre. (Simple: the alarm that tells you if something is scary, fun, or safe.)
regions.role: Tags experiences with emotional value such as fear, reward, or urgency, so the brain prioritises what matters. (Simple: adds feelings so you notice what's important.)
regions.exampleActivity: Flags a busy road as risky, or a treat as rewarding.

regions.name: Prefrontal Cortex
regions.shortDescription: The brain's control centre for planning and decisions. (Simple: the thinker that makes the final choice.)
regions.role: Integrates sensory evidence, memory, and emotion with your goals to plan and choose actions. (Simple: puts together what you see, remember, and feel to decide what to do.)
regions.exampleActivity: Weighs whether to eat the apple, or to wait before crossing a road.

regions.name: Motor Cortex
regions.shortDescription: The region that commands voluntary movement. (Simple: tells your body to move.)
regions.role: Converts decisions into precise signals that drive coordinated muscle movement. (Simple: turns your choice into a move, like reaching or stepping.)
regions.exampleActivity: Signals your hand to reach for the apple, or your legs to step back.

regions.name: Long-Term Memory Network
regions.shortDescription: A network spread across the cortex that stores lasting knowledge. (Simple: the library that keeps what you've learned for a long time.)
regions.role: Holds durable, connected patterns of knowledge that can be strengthened, weakened, and reactivated. (Simple: stores what you know so you can use it again days or years later.)
regions.exampleActivity: Stores that apples are sweet, safe, and familiar.
